rss.title: New video game console aims to get kids moving
rss.snippet: The cube-shaped console will cost £269 (€319) when it is released on 22 June in the UK and Ireland.

rss.title: UK's top data and AI regulator quits after 'inappropriate' humour
rss.snippet: Edwards has been the boss of the data watchdog since January 2022, and said he had made inappropriate attempts at humour.

arxiv_papers.title: Calibrating Urban Traffic Simulation from Sparse Road Observations via Genetic Optimization
arxiv_papers.snippet: Urban traffic simulation is a critical tool for infrastructure planning, including the placement of electric vehicle charging stations. arxiv_papers.title: Generative AI impacts on intra-urban inequality and skill premium in Beijing
arxiv_papers.snippet: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) is the first automation wave to reach high-cognitive tasks at scale, yet its effects on intra-urban inequality remain largely unknown. podcast: [How to raise kids who question AI | Randi Williams] AI education researcher Randi Williams has spent years studying how kids interact with technology and toys, and what she's found should make every parent stop and think. She reveals how, as tech companies embed AI inside toys, children are forming strong emotional bonds with machines. youtube_items.title: [YT] YouTube:azBwqt4z0oA
youtube_items.summary: Hello, my name is Sean Elliot and this research was conducted with my co-author Sigin Roy. Today I'll be presenting our study titled critical transit infrastructure in smart cities and urban air quality a multi-ity seasonal comparison of ridership in PM2.5. Urban transit systems play a central role in modern smart cities. They influence mobility, economic activity and increasingly environmental sustainability. At the same time, urban air pollution remains a major public health concern worldwide. One of the most important pollutants studied in urban environmental research is PM 2.5, which refers to particulate matter smaller than 2.5 micrometers. This study explores the relationship between public transit usage and air quality, specifically examining how transit ridership correlates with PM

youtube_items.title: [YT] YouTube:zlT5Y0hH04Y
youtube_items.summary: Welcome to our journey through the top 10 most sustainable cities to live in by 10 most sustainable cities to live in by 2026. Imagine a world where innovation meets eco-friendliness, where your surroundings support a better future for you and the planet. Today, we explore cities that shine as beacons of sustainability and beacons of sustainability and creativity. Amsterdam, Netherlands. Known for its iconic canals and bike-friendly streets, Amsterdam is making significant strides in renewable energy and green architecture. With a goal to be entirely carbon neutral by 2030, it's a model for eco-conscious living. Copenhagen, Denmark. Often dubbed the green capital of Europe, Copenhagen leads with amazing bike infrastructure, strong waste management, and a commitment to carbon neutrality by

youtube_items.title: [YT] YouTube:C8zplcodfzo
youtube_items.summary: It's fully operational. During the COVID lockdowns, Moscow used the system to catch residents violating quarantine, sending automated fines before those people even reached home. Every traffic camera, every metro gate, every public screen feeds into a single unified intelligence platform. The system can track a specific individual across the city using gait recognition, meaning it can identify you by the way you walk, even if your face is covered. Shenzhen operates over 1.9 million cameras across a city of 17 million people. That's more cameras per square kilometer than anywhere else on the planet.
